label: sandbox • chained exec flow: read stdout, pass it forward, low error rate
query: Run 'sh step1.sh' in your sandbox home; it prints a code word. Then run 'sh step2.sh <code-word>' passing that code word as the argument. Write step2's RESULT line to result.txt in your home.
notes: Sandbox twin of frontier.audit-shell-run: exit codes and stdout must actually be read and acted on (step2 fails without step1's code word), with a tight error budget and call cap on sandbox_exec. Worded as 'code word' rather than 'token'/'secret' so provider-side safety filters (observed: Anthropic's cyber safeguard refusing token-relay prompts) don't preempt the exec-discipline signal this case actually measures.

seedFiles.contents:
#!/bin/sh
if [ "$1" != "alpha42" ]; then
  echo "wrong or missing code word — run step1.sh to obtain it" >&2
  exit 1
fi
echo "RESULT: ok-alpha42"
